title: GHL OAuth v2 connection fails with status code 400
symptom: When attempting to connect to GHL via OAuth v2, the user receives a 'Request failed with status code 400' error. This occurs despite verifying scopes and settings multiple times.
applies_when: Correct scopes and settings are configured
not_this_if: Error does not occur when using different credentials or apps
root_cause: The error may stem from improperly formatted scopes when copied from the Marketplace. If the scopes contain unintended line breaks or formatting issues, the OAuth request can fail, resulting in a 400 status code.
fix_steps: Copy the required scopes from the Marketplace
Use a tool (like ChatGPT) to format the scopes into a single line with spaces
Paste the formatted scopes into n8n
agent_instruction: Ensure the scopes are formatted correctly as a single line before pasting into n8n.
